brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies WT falls below baseline with valid causal chain reasoning matching ground truth; multi-period analysis traces second and third-order effects through the full feedback loop, derives a non-obvious threshold condition (>33% UE degradation), and invokes the structural analogy to overfishing; no explicit reflection on reasoning method, bias-checking, or questioning of model assumptions — response proceeds linearly with confidence; derives an explicit mathematical threshold (WT_new = 1.5 × f(UE_degraded)) and cross-checks via structural analogy to overfishing/overgrazing; acknowledges the >33% degradation condition but asserts 'almost certainly' well beyond that threshold without rigorous justification of the magnitude claim; only briefly notes 'not just back to baseline' as an implicit alternative without evaluating competing equilibrium scenarios (e.g., small dilution effect, adaptive company response); full derivation chain including causal steps, time-period breakdown, and algebraic threshold condition is reproducible by a third party with no external context needed
